title: PIE Root Cognate & Correspondence Entry
description: Schema for a single reconstructed PIE root, its cognate set across daughter languages, positional sound correspondences, and verification metadata.

gloss.description: Short English meaning of the root (e.g. 'hand').

gloss_source.description: Where the gloss value came from. 'iecor' if IE-CoR's Root_Gloss column had a value; 'primary_source_fallback' if IE-CoR's was empty and this was pulled from pie_roots.json instead; 'none' if neither source had one. Kept separate and explicit so IE-CoR-native and fallback glosses are never silently blended.

verification_tier.description: Which of the three confidence tiers this entry belongs to.

cognate_set.description: Attested forms across daughter languages that make up this cognate set.

lang.description: ISO 639 or Wiktionary lang_code for the daughter language (e.g. 'en', 'got').

correspondence_sets.description: Positional (aligned-slot) sound correspondences across the cognate set. Use 'Ø' as an explicit value where a language has no reflex in that slot, rather than omitting the language key.

position.description: Aligned slot label (e.g. 'onset', 'medial', 'coda', 'stressed_vowel', 'unstressed_ending'). Free text but should be consistent across entries for the same root shape.

values.description: Map of lang code -> IPA symbol (or 'Ø' for absence) at this position. Keys should match lang codes used in cognate_set.

phonetic_environment.description: Optional conditioning environment that explains why this correspondence differs from others involving the same phoneme (e.g. 'stressed', 'unstressed', 'word-final', 'before_consonant').

note.description: Free-text clarification, e.g. explaining an exception or an ending's grammatical function.

sound_law_tags.description: References into a separate curated sound-law table (e.g. Grimm's Law, Verner's Law) that license one or more of the correspondence_sets rows above.

confidence.description: Provenance and confidence metadata distinguishing gloss-matching from verified etymological descent.

source.description: Primary data source for this entry (e.g. 'IE-CoR', 'Wiktionary/kaikki', 'LingRex').

cognacy_verified_by.description: Who/what verified that this is genuine shared descent rather than surface resemblance.

gloss_match_only.description: True if this entry is only a meaning match (gloss overlap) and has NOT been verified as true etymological descent. Must be false for iecor_verified entries.
